page: Amazon Web Services r5a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting simultaneous multithreading with 2.0 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tasks scoring in the bottom 10%. However, the high core count compensates in parallel workloads, yielding average results in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is a notable strength, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50%. Passmark CPU encryption tests also show strong performance in the top 25%.

The qualitative cost profile of the r5a.24xlarge is defined by its high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier single-thread CPU speeds. The lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its hardware profile, this instance is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, big data processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM footprints without demanding extreme single-core processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services r5a.24xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7571 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it features a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate poor single-core performance, placing in the bottom 10% for single-threaded tasks. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks and database operations fall within the middle 50%, while cached memory bandwidth and encryption tests rank in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that can leverage parallel processing. It is suited for in-memory databases, distributed caching, and large-scale enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.24xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, designed specifically for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six virtual cores and seven hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors.
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz.
Strong cached memory bandwidth and CPU encryption performance.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and parallel enterprise workloads.
